title: One State

{{Audio|file=Hearts of Iron IV Red Flood OST - Anthem of One State (slowed by SetaCraft).mp3|align=center|volume=0.5|loop=no|mono=yes}}
The One State,
Our Gehenna
{{Fullscreen|padding=0px|show=
Hell does not need to be a Lake of Fire.
|hide= Close.|content=
[[File:Redfloodeddamnation.jpg|800px]]
{{Background|file=Level 0 TheLobby.jpg|opacity=20%}}
[[File:Level_3999.jpg|600px]]
[[File:Level_1.jpg|600px]]
Ahh, the Backrooms.

This alternate dimension separate from baseline reality has got you covered for a perilous demise at nearly any moment.

There are some levels in the Backrooms that can almost end an unsuspecting soul near-instantly, from those nightmarish pylons, corrupted paradises, and radioactive wastelands.
{{Background|file=Level_%21%5E2.webp|opacity=40%}}
[[File:Infernumdogattemptsnumber.webp|600px]]
[[File:Deathmoth1.png|400px]]
However...

a level doesn't need an entire battalion of entities hunting you or environmental hazards populating every square inch
for it to be the most dangerous of all.
{{Background|file=SubSpktbbbb.jpg|opacity=20%}}
[[File:Viber_image_2023-06-01_00-41-37-149.jpg|600px]]
[[File:830_Hopeful_Whisper.png|300px]]
The Backrooms is no stranger to torture, as seen by its many psychologically detrimental levels and endless,
non-Euclidean labyrinths.

So it's no surprise if the most dangerous level of them all is one purely focused on torture.
Enter...
The One State, Our Gehenna
[[File:We-illustration.png]]
A giant, neverending city filled with glass buildings, to maximize surveillance.
In between these transparent monoliths we see swathes of regular humans and Facelings. You then notice that all the humans are walking the exact same paths, down to the minute details.

Yet, they aren't marching a parade, this is normal life for them.
Then, you notice something odd.
Your trusty water tumbler that you bring with no longer has your name etched in its metal, replaced instead with a random string of letters and numbers. You think to yourself that this is another Backrooms phenomenon.
You brush it off and naively hope that it will go away when you depart the level.
Every minute detail is eerily identical. The entire level is seemingly drenched in near-absolute monotony.
Even the dust on the ground is identical to one another.
Almost everything is completely identical to one another. Your presence contrasts like a stain in a white cloth.
But, as seen with your tumbler, your existence will slowly unite with the level's absolute monotony.
Even if you have seen them for only the first time, you eerily and accurately replicate the movements of the robotic masses around you. Suddenly, you notice that your outfit now perfectly matches that of the robotic, yet human mass.
Things continue turning for the worse. Your anomalous water tumbler, along with all your other belongings simply vanished.
Your brain tells you to panic, but despite this, you still follow the unhumanly orderly positions and movements of the masses, despite the strength of your instincts.
Your body morphs into the surrounding masses around you. While changes are slight, you feel excruciating pain in your body as it changes its structure.
But despite screaming in pain mentally, you continue to ignore these body signals, and continue marching towards a factory.
As you enter the industrial premises, you see endless, repeating facilities, machines, and workers.
As you traverse the long, unending repetitiveness, you notice an empty space between two workers, which you now fill in.
As you walk towards the empty space, you are struck with a migraine. Suddenly, you are no longer the curious and fascinated explorer you are.
The One State, the Great Machine, has taken another unsuspecting soul.

You are now reduced to a vegetative state, feeling the pain and suffering brought by factory work.
An adventurer once glorious.
He who feels the soft winds of 48.
He who basks in the waters of 330.
He who reaps the glory of slaying countless beasts.
He who lusts for a fine maiden.
He who braved the Tragedy of 116.
He who stood before the Negative's crown jewel.

He who flew too close to the Sun, and spends his eternity damned to the Ultimate Machine.
A once lively wanderer, stripped of all human emotion.
There is nothing anymore for he who is unfortunate.
No more humanity in one.
Not even a will.
Just plain, consciousness.

All this to maximize production values.
And what for? What is even being produced in the first place?
Is this where new levels are created?
Or are they just creating nothing of importance?
Is there even any manufacturing going on?
Even in the deepest, darkest corners of the Backrooms, you still have a will to survive.
The will to dash towards safety, the will to stay on high alert in deadzones, the freedom to think smart to ensure survival. Or you could choose death.
The One State is absent of these luxuries.
Once someone enters, there is no egress from the constant, repetitive factory work, the obnoxious ear-shattering noises coming from every direction, and their vegetative consciousness.
And those who do escape, if they somehow do, their everlasting assailment continues on, with their wretched metamorphosis...
The Ultimate Machine,
the One State,
a fate worse than death,
amongst the incomprehensible labyrinth.
[[File:Overlayed.png|link=]]
